result.stdout:
Description:
  liz - a dotnet-tool to analyze the licenses of your project(s)

Usage:
  Liz.Tool <targetFile> [options]

Arguments:
  <targetFile>  The target file to analyze. Can be a solution (.sln) or a project (.csproj, .fsproj) file

Options:
  -l, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log-level which describes what kind of messages are displayed when running the tool [default: Information]
  -i, --include-transitive                                               If transitive dependencies should be included or not [default: False]
  -sd, --suppress-print-details                                          If printing the license and package-reference details should be suppressed or not [default: False]
  -si, --suppress-print-issues                                           If printing the license-information issues should be suppressed or not [default: False]
  -sb, --suppress-progressbar                                            If displaying the progressbar should be suppressed or not. Can help when debugging errors or is used in a CI/CD Pipeline [default: False]
  -td, --license-type-definitions <license-type-definitions>             Provide a path to a JSON-File (local or remote - remote will be downloaded automatically if available) providing license-type-definitions which describe license-types by providing inclusive/exclusive license-text snippets []
  -um, --url-type-mapping <url-type-mapping>                             Provide a path to a JSON-file (local or remote - remote will be downloaded automatically if available) containing a mapping from license-url (key) to license-type (value) for licenses whose license-type could not be determined []
  -w, --whitelist <whitelist>                                            Provide a path to a JSON-File (local or remote - remote will be downloaded automatically if available) containing a list of license-types, which are the only ones allowed, when validating the determined license-types. Any license-type which is not in the whitelist will cause the validation to fail. '--whitelist' and '--blacklist' are mutually exclusive! []
  -b, --blacklist <blacklist>                                            Provide a path to a JSON-File (local or remote - remote will be downloaded automatically if available) containing a list of license-types, which are the only ones disallowed, when validating the determined license-types. Any license-type that is the same as within that blacklist will cause the validation to fail. Any other license-type is allowed. '--whitelist' and '--blacklist' are mutually exclusive! []
  -et, --export-texts <export-texts>                                     A path to a directory to where the determined license-texts will be exported. Each license-text will be written to an individual file with the file-name being: "<package-name>-<package-version>.txt". If the license-text is the content of a website, the contents will be written into an ".html" file instead []
  -ej, --export-json <export-json>                                       A path to a JSON-file t which the determined license- and package-information will be exported. All the information will be written to a single JSON-file. If the file already exists it will be overwritten. []
  -t, --timeout <timeout>                                                The timeout for a request (i.e. to get the license text from a website). After this amount of time a request will be considered as failed and aborted. []
  -pe, --project-excludes <project-excludes>                             A path to a JSON-File (local or remote - remote will be downloaded automatically if available) containing a list of glob-patterns to exclude certain projects. A project will be excluded when it matches at least one glob-pattern. The pattern will be matched against the absolute path of the project-file. All available patterns can be found here: https://github.com/dazinator/DotNet.Glob/tree/3.1.3#patterns []
  -de, --dependency-excludes <dependency-excludes>                       A path to a JSON-File (local or remote - remote will be downloaded automatically if available) containing a list of glob-patterns to exclude certain packages. A package will be excluded when it matches at least one glob-pattern. The pattern will be matched against the name of the package. All available patterns can be found here: https://github.com/dazinator/DotNet.Glob/tree/3.1.3#patterns []
  --version                                                              Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                                         Show help and usage information

Description:
  liz - a dotnet-tool to analyze the licenses of your project(s)

Usage:
  Liz.Tool <targetFile> [options]

Arguments:
  <targetFile>  The target file to analyze. Can be a solution (.sln) or a project (.csproj, .fsproj) file

Options:
  -l, --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  The log-level which describes what kind of messages are displayed when running the tool [default: Information]

  -w, --whitelist <whitelist>                                            Provide a path to a JSON-File (local or remote - remote will be downloaded automatically if available) containing a list of license-types, which are the only ones allowed, when validating the determined license-types. Any license-type which is not in the whitelist will cause the validation to fail. '--whitelist' and '--blacklist' are mutually exclusive! []

Any license-type that is the same as within that blacklist will cause the validation to fail. Any other license-type is allowed. '--whitelist' and '--blacklist' are mutually exclusive! []
  -et, --export-texts <export-texts>                                     A path to a directory to where the determined license-texts will be exported. Each license-text will be written to an individual file with the file-name being: "<package-name>-<package-version>.txt".   -ej, --export-json <export-json>                                       A path to a JSON-file t which the determined license- and package-information will be exported. All the information will be written to a single JSON-file. If the file already exists it will be overwritten. []
  -t, --timeout <timeout>                                                The timeout for a request (i.e. to get the license text from a website). After this amount of time a request will be considered as failed and aborted. []
  -pe, --project-excludes <project-excludes>                             A path to a JSON-File (local or remote - remote will be downloaded automatically if available) containing a list of glob-patterns to exclude certain projects. A project will be excluded when it matches at least one glob-pattern. The pattern will be matched against the absolute path of the project-file. All available patterns can be found here: https://github.com/dazinator/DotNet.Glob/tree/3.1.3#patterns []
  -de, --dependency-excludes <dependency-excludes>                       A path to a JSON-File (local or remote - remote will be downloaded automatically if available) containing a list of glob-patterns to exclude certain packages. A package will be excluded when it matches at least one glob-pattern. The pattern will be matched against the name of the package. All available patterns can be found here: https://github.com/dazinator/DotNet.Glob/tree/3.1.3#patterns []
  --version                                                              Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                                         Show help and usage information
